name: gitwhy
purpose: Code archaeology: for a file region, blame the lines, collect the commits that shaped them, condense their messages, and pull the linked PR/issue references with dates and authors - one JSON answer to 'why is this code like this?'. --churn surfaces hotspot files (high change frequency x low coverage) as a risk report.
reach_for_me_when: you are about to grep-blame-log spelunk to understand why some code exists
you need the PR/issue that introduced or last changed a region
you want a risk report of files that change often but are barely tested

commands.name: explain
commands.summary: Blame a region -> commits, authors, dates, and linked PR/issue refs

commands.name: churn
commands.summary: Rank files by change frequency; --coverage-json adds a risk score
